titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Mary Anne Curley, 1 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926), the Assistant Under Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Guest Churchill, Lord Wimborne. The recipient was Mrs. Mary Anne Curley. In this letter O'Farrell informs Mrs. Curley that her children are not detained in Industrial schools and that the Lord Justices cannot interfere with the Rathdown Guardians and their exercise of power.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3730

correspAction.placeName: No. 3 Caseys Cottages, Little Bray, County Wicklow, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
1st March,1916.
No. 3730. Madam, In reply to your letter of the 27th ultimo,
I am directed by the Lords Justices to inform you
that your children are not detained in Industrial
Schools, and that Their Excellencies cannot inter-
fere with the Rathdown Guardians in the exercise
of their powers in the matter. I am,
Madam,
Your obedient Servant, E O'Farrell Mrs. Mary Anne Curley,
No.3 Caseys Cottages,
Little Bray,
Co. Wicklow.
